topic_title: Interpreting AI output variation — what it tells you about reliability

section_3_learning_objectives.note: Section 3 now contains 6 learning objectives. Threshold cleared.

topic_scope.details: All material — including the new sixth learning objective — is within the headline scope: interpreting AI output variation as a reliability signal. The six objectives map directly onto sections 5.1 (output variation as signal), 5.2 (temperature-adjusted reading), 5.3 (four-quadrant framework), 5.5 (measurement workflow), 5.4 (task type), and 5.6 (consistency vs correctness) respectively. No off-topic concepts taught at depth. Accuracy, precision, and recall from prior topics are applied (not re-taught) in section 5.5.

verdict: PASS — all 6 checks pass. Section 3 (Learning Objectives) now counts 114 words across 6 objectives, clearing the 100-word minimum that caused the cycle-1 hard failure.
